hints.date: 'date' means 'give' but does not appear in this phrase

hints.to relax: While related in English, 'laxare' in this context means 'to let down/loosen' the nets

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 2nd plural would be 'laxatis' — 'Laxate' is imperative plural
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'laxetis' — 'Laxate' is the imperative plural
hints.infinitive: Infinitive would be 'laxare' — 'Laxate' ending in -ate is imperative plural

hints.laudo, laudare, laudavi, laudatum: These are principal parts of 'laudo' (to praise)
hints.lavo, lavare, lavi, lautum: These are principal parts of 'lavo' (to wash)
hints.lego, legere, legi, lectum: These are principal parts of 'lego' (to read/gather)

hints.nominative: Nominative plural neuter is also 'retia', but here it is the direct object of 'laxate'
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'retibus' — 'retia' is accusative plural neuter
hints.vocative: You cannot address nets — 'retia' is the accusative direct object

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'retis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'retis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'retis' ends in -is
